fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Washington Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity washington, how to transfer pharmacist license to washington, pharmacist compact washington, washington pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission
board.mailing_address:
Washington State Board of Pharmacy
Department of Health
P.O. Box 47852
Olympia, WA 98504-7852

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Washington.

steps.title: Create a SAW Account
steps.description: Register for a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account at secureaccess.wa.gov and add the 'DOH Health Professions' service to begin your application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log into your SAW account, access the DOH Health Professions service, and submit the pharmacist license application form, providing all required personal and professional details.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your ACPE-accredited pharmacy program to be sent directly to the Board. Upload or submit proof of 1,500 internship hours as required within the SAW portal or as directed by the Board.

steps.title: Pass Examinations
steps.description: Ensure your NAPLEX and MPJE scores are submitted directly from the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) to the Washington State Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Remit all required application and examination fees as instructed through the SecureAccess Washington (SAW) portal to finalize your application submission.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Washington pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Washington have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Washington. To work in Washington, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Washington?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Washington
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Washington, you must register and apply online through the Department of Health's SecureAccess Washington (SAW) portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Go to secureaccess.wa.gov and click 'Register' to create a new SAW account.
2. Follow the prompts to set up your username and password.
3. After logging in, click 'Add a Service' and search for 'DOH Health Professions'.
4. Select 'DOH Health Professions' and follow the instructions to link it to your account.
5. Once added, you can access the online application system for your license.
